information.note: Currently hue 241 - unintentional drift from brand hue 261. Target: migrate onto hue 261 at info weight so the system carries one blue. Treat 241 as legacy.

display.purpose: text-2xl (1.5rem/600). Page titles. Fixed scale step, never fluid clamp.

body.purpose: text-sm (0.875rem/400). Workhorse UI/data size - one step below web default for density. Prose caps 65-75ch; tables may run 120ch+.

label.purpose: text-xs (0.75rem/500). Field labels, badges, meta, dense data. Sentence case, not tracked uppercase.

mono.purpose: JetBrains Mono at text-sm/text-xs steps. Machine truth: code, span/trace IDs, timestamps, raw logs, KQL.

motion.name: button-press
motion.purpose: Tactile press signature on all buttons.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Signal-blue primary action with tactile press feedback.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: var(--color-fillBrand-strong, oklch(56.7% 0.239 261)); color: #fff; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: none; border-radius: 0.4rem; font: 500 0.875rem/1 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 75ms ease-out, background 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(52% 0.239 261); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(56.7% 0.239 261); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active:not(:disabled) { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Neutral secondary action; hairline stroke, no fill.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: oklch(16.7% 0.086 263 / 0.85); padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: 1px solid oklch(25.2% 0.146 264 / 0.14); border-radius: 0.4rem; font: 500 0.875rem/1 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease-out, transform 75ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: oklch(29.2% 0.174 264 / 0.05); } .ds-btn-ghost:active { transform: scale(0.98); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Field with hairline border; focus shifts to Signal Blue.
components.css: .ds-input { background: oklch(99.1% 0.002 247); color: oklch(14.4% 0.069 263 / 0.9); padding: 0.375rem 0.625rem; border: 1px solid oklch(25.2% 0.146 264 / 0.12); border-radius: 0.4rem; font: 400 0.875rem/1.45 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; transition: border-color 150ms ease-out, box-shadow 150ms ease-out; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(21.2% 0.117 264 / 0.55); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: oklch(56.7% 0.239 261); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(56.7% 0.239 261 / 0.2); }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Compact status label; solid, outline, or ghost. Status color is semantic.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.25rem; padding: 0.125rem 0.5rem; border-radius: 8px; font: 500 0.75rem/1 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-badge-error { background: oklch(51.3% 0.197 21 / 0.1); color: oklch(51.3% 0.197 21); border: 1px solid oklch(51.3% 0.197 21 / 0.2); } .ds-badge-success { background: oklch(47.8% 0.130 162 / 0.1); color: oklch(47.8% 0.130 162); }

components.name: Nav Tab
components.description: Tab/nav link; inactive is muted, active handled by morph swap.
components.css: .ds-tabs { display: inline-flex; gap: 0.25rem; border-bottom: 1px solid oklch(25.2% 0.146 264 / 0.1); } .ds-tab { padding: 0.5rem 1rem; color: oklch(16.7% 0.086 263 / 0.65); font: 500 0.875rem/1 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; cursor: pointer; transition: color 150ms ease-out, border-color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-tab:hover { color: oklch(14.4% 0.069 263 / 0.9); } .ds-tab-active { color: oklch(56.7% 0.239 261); border-bottom-color: oklch(56.7% 0.239 261); }

narrative.overview: Monoscope is read like a cockpit, not browsed like a website. Its user is an on-call engineer at 3 AM following a shared trace link with an error clock ticking, so the interface behaves like an instrument panel: every readout carries meaning, every color is a gauge rather than paint, and density is a form of respect. The emotional target is relief, never spectacle. Color is strictly signal; the reference is Datadog APM stripped of its glossier edges. The system is light-first with full dark parity, and shared views are first-class.
keyCharacteristics: Instrument-panel density: tight within rows, generous between groups.
Color is signal, never decoration - brand blue for action, semantic hues for state.
OKLCH semantic-token system with strict light/dark parity.
Inter for UI and data; JetBrains Mono for code, IDs, and raw telemetry.
Tonal surfaces + 1px strokes carry depth; shadows are reserved for floating layers.

rules.name: The Signal Rule
rules.body: Color is signal, not decoration. Brand blue means clickable; red means error; green means success; a service or status hue means identity. No meaning, no color.

rules.name: The One-Blue Rule
rules.body: There is a single blue hue: the brand's 261. It carries interaction at full strength (fillBrand) and informational state at a lighter weight (fillInformation) - the same hue, never a second one. fillInformation currently drifts to hue 241 (legacy); the target is to migrate it onto 261.

rules.name: The Machine-Truth Rule
rules.body: Machine-emitted content (span IDs, status codes, logs, queries) is set in JetBrains Mono at the same text-sm/text-xs steps as its neighbours; human chrome is Inter. The font tells the reader which is which.

rules.name: The Fixed-Scale Rule
rules.body: Every type size is a named step on the Tailwind scale (text-2xs through text-2xl for chrome; text-3xl+ reserved for rare hero moments). No fluid clamp headings, no one-off rem values, no separate display typeface. Weight and step create hierarchy.

rules.name: The Float-Only Shadow Rule
rules.body: A shadow means this floats above the page. Resting surfaces earn depth from tone and stroke, never from a drop shadow.

dos: Do treat color as signal - brand blue for interaction, semantic hues for state, service/status hues for identity. No meaning, no color.
Do pack density where power users scan: tight within rows, generous between logical groups.
Do keep one blue hue (brand 261); info is that hue at a lighter weight, distinguished by strength, not by a rival blue.
Do set machine-emitted content in JetBrains Mono; human chrome in Inter.
Do convey depth with tonal surface steps and 1px strokes; reserve shadows for floating layers.
Do verify every token and state in both light and dark - dark parity is required.
Do give every error state a second signal beyond color. WCAG AA is the floor.
Do express presentational state with Tailwind variants so it survives HTMX morphs.
donts: Don't use playful illustrations, mascots, or decorative icons above headings.
Don't ship neon-on-dark, glassmorphism as decoration, gradient text, or cartoony empty states.
Don't add border-left / border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't warm the neutral base toward cream/sand - it is faintly cooled toward the brand hue.
Don't introduce a display font, fluid clamp heading, or one-off rem size; every type size is a named step on the scale.
Don't add a second blue hue; the system has one blue (261), and info is that hue at a lighter weight.
Don't put a resting shadow on a card that isn't floating.
Don't reinvent standard affordances for flavor.
Don't reach for a modal as the first thought - exhaust inline and progressive-disclosure alternatives first.
